config_files.notes: Primary game config. Overwritten by in-game settings menu. Edit only while LoL is fully closed and the Riot client is not patching.
Stores HUD, minimap, and UI settings. Not graphics-critical but relevant for accessibility settings (UI scale, colorblind mode).

keys.notes: Low/off shadows are the competitive standard in LoL. Shadows make it slightly harder to distinguish champion positions on some terrain textures.

Low effects quality reduces visual clutter from high-ability-spam scenarios (ARAM, late-game teamfights with multiple AoE champions). Also aids readability in crowded teamfights for players with visual processing preferences.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Enable Deuteranopia, Tritanopia, or Protanopia mode as appropriate. Changes team and ability color schemes to improve distinction.
manual_only_settings.reason: Colorblind mode is stored in PersistedSettings.json but is most safely set in-game to avoid JSON format errors.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Off for maximum FPS. On for visual quality — LoL is not demanding enough on mid/high hardware that AA causes meaningful FPS loss.

notes: League of Legends is one of the least hardware-demanding major PC games. On modern hardware (2018+), GPU settings have minimal FPS impact — CPU speed and RAM latency matter more for the game's logic tick rate.
The Riot engine config (game.cfg) uses standard INI syntax, making it one of the simpler profiles to edit.
Low effects quality is particularly beneficial in ARAM (All Random All Mid) which has the highest sustained ability density of any LoL game mode.
The Riot Vanguard anti-cheat runs for Valorant but not for League of Legends. LoL uses a different anti-cheat subsystem. Config file edits are safe for both games.
